instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Term Insurance Benefits for Nominees
Added Word Count: 334
URL: https://www.bajajallianzlife.com/life-insurance-guide/term/term-insurance-benefits-for-nominees.html
Nomination is one of the many term insurance benefits that you can take advantage of when you purchase a term insurance plan. In fact, it is crucial to the core idea of term insurance itself, because the very purpose of purchasing a term plan is to ensure that the financial benefits offered under the policy protect your dependents. So, they will receive the benefits if you nominate them under the term insurance plan.
But if you’re a beginner to the whole concept of term insurance, you may be unsure of what a nominee is. Let’s find out what the term ‘nominee’ means and the term insurance benefits for nominees.
What is a Term Insurance Nominee?
A term insurance nominee is an essential part of every life insurance plan. And a term insurance plan is no different. When a person purchases term insurance, they have the right to appoint a nominee. This term insurance nominee is the person who will receive the term insurance benefits in case of the life assured’s demise.
Policyholders also have the option to nominate multiple beneficiaries in the proposal form. In that case, the policyholder also has to specify the percentages according to which the sum assured will be divided between the nominees assigned to the policy.
Generally, term insurance nominees are family members who are dependent on the policyholder. This is because in case of the policyholder’s demise, the death benefits will be paid out to the nominee directly. That will make it easier for the dependent family members to cushion the financial blow of losing their sole or primary source of income.
Assigning a nominee is a quick and simple process, particularly in the case of online term insurance. You simply need to fill in the relevant details and submit the information online.
5 features of Term Insurance that makes life easier on the Term Insurance Nominee
Term insurance offers many benefits for nominees. In fact, most of the features of term insurance plans are designed to ensure that the nominees have financial security in place. Here are three key features of term insurance that make life easier on the term insurance nominee.
Death Benefits
Many families rely on just one or two members for their source of income. When the sole or the primary earning member of a family passes away unexpectedly, it may leave the surviving members without any financial support to fall back on. A nominee can be of great help here. That is because term plans pay out the sum assured as death benefits to the nominee, thereby giving them the financial cushion needed during such uncertain times. With this financial safety net to fall back on, the surviving family members can continue to meet their daily expenses and fulfil their life goals as planned.
Tax Benefits
For nominees who are worried about the tax implications of receiving the death benefits offered by term plans, there’s reason to relax.
As per section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act, 1961, the sum received as death benefits under a term plan is exempt from tax, subject to the conditions specified therein.
For example, say a person purchases a term insurance plan for a certain premium amount with a sum assured of Rs. 1 crore and a policy term of 30 years, and names his spouse as the nominee. 5 years later, the person passes away unexpectedly. In this case, the nominee - that is, the person’s spouse - receives the sum assured of Rs. 1 crore. This sum is tax-free, provided the premium and sum-assured amount meet the criteria specified as mentioned in section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act, 1961.
Flexible Pay Out Options
Many term plans also offer flexible payout options, meaning that the nominees can receive the benefits as a lump sum amount or as periodic payouts. These payouts can also be customized as per the nominee’s needs and can be a combination of a lump sum pay-out and monthly income, or a lump sum pay-out with increasing monthly income depending upon the featured of the product chosen. Based on the financial needs of the surviving members of the family, the pay-out options can be chosen.
Freedom from Debt
In the unfortunate event that the life assured passes away, leaving behind loans, the death benefit can be used to make a payment on those loans. Consequently, the family members can get financial respite from the burden of paying outstanding loans. This can give them financial stability.
Financial Security
One of the important features of a term plan is offering financial security in the absence of the life assured. The death benefit received from the policy can take care of the lifestyle expenses of the family members. If you choose an adequate sum assured, the death benefit can also help in meeting your family’s financial goals in your absence.
This can offer financial security in the knowledge that your family members might not face financial troubles in your absence.
Key Takeaways
A term insurance plan is a protection-oriented life insurance plan that can provide financial security to your family in your absence.
Nomination allows easy settlement of the death benefit.
The death benefit of a term plan can help your family meet its expenses, pay off debts and fulfil their financial goals.
Conclusion
With so many benefits, term insurance plans are one of the simplest and most cost-effective products available in the insurance market. They don't just offer financial security—they can also facilitate a prompt and hassle-free transfer of funds to the designated nominee, eliminating unnecessary delays or operational complications. If you have dependents in your family, make sure you nominate the right person or persons when you purchase your term insurance plan so your loved ones receive prompt assistance and are able to maintain their standard of living without significant financial hardships.
FAQs
What is the benefit of a nominee?
A nominee receives the death benefit from the term plan. The nominee can direct the benefit to the designated beneficiaries, thereby safeguarding the financial interests of dependents in the event of the life assured’s death.
What are the disadvantages of a nominee?
Who cannot be nominated?
Usually, there is no restriction on nominating an individual to the life insurance policy.
Can we add nominees to term insurance?
Yes, you have the flexibility to add or update nominees both at the time of purchase and throughout the policy tenure.
Disclaimer
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajlifeinsurance.com) carefully before concluding a sale. Bajaj Life Insurance Limited (Formerly known as Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Limited) Reg. Office Address: Bajaj Insurance House, Airport Road, Yerawada, Pune - 411006. CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959,  call us on Customer Care No. 020-6712 1212 , mail us on: customercare@bajajlife.com. The Logo of Bajaj Life Insurance Limited is provided on the basis of license given by Bajaj Finserv Ltd. to use its “Bajaj” Logo.
Tax benefits as per prevailing Section 10(10D) and Section 80C (under old tax regime) of the Income Tax Act shall apply. You are requested to consult your tax consultant and obtain independent advice for eligibility before claiming any benefit under the policy

Added Word Count: 334
URL: https://www.bajajallianzlife.com/life-insurance-guide/term/term-insurance-benefits-for- nominees.html

5 features of Term Insurance that makes life easier on the Term Insurance Nominee
Term insurance offers many benefits for nominees. In fact, most of the features of term insurance plans are designed to ensure that the nominees have financial security in place. Here are three key features of term insurance that make life easier on the term insurance nominee.
Death Benefits
Many families rely on just one or two members for their source of income. the surviving family members can continue to meet their daily expenses and fulfil their life goals as planned.
Tax Benefits
For nominees who are worried about the tax implications of receiving the death benefits offered by term plans, there’s reason to relax.
As per section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act, 1961, the sum received as death benefits under a term plan is exempt from tax, subject to the conditions specified therein.
For example, say a person purchases a term insurance plan for a certain premium amount with a sum assured of Rs. 1 crore and a policy term of 30 years, and names his spouse as the nominee. 5 years later, the person passes away unexpectedly. In this case, the nominee - that is, the person’s spouse - receives the sum assured of Rs. 1 crore. This sum is tax-free, provided the premium and sum-assured amount meet the criteria specified as mentioned in section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act, 1961.
Flexible Pay Out Options
Many term plans also offer flexible payout options, meaning that the nominees can receive the benefits as a lump sum amount or as periodic payouts. These payouts can also be customized as per the nominee’s needs and can be a combination of a lump sum pay-out and monthly income, or a lump sum pay-out with increasing monthly income depending upon the featured of the product chosen. Based on the financial needs of the surviving members of the family, the pay-out options can be chosen.
Easier Debt Management
In the unfortunate event that the life assured passes away and there are outstanding loans, the death benefit can be used to clear such loans. Consequently, the family members may get financial respite from the burden of paying outstanding loans. This can give them financial stability.
Financial Security
One of the important features of a term plan is offering financial security in the absence of the life assured. The death benefit received from the policy can take care of the lifestyle expenses of the family members. If you choose an adequate sum assured, the death benefit can also help in meeting your family’s financial goals in your absence.
This can offer financial security in the knowledge that your family members might not face financial troubles in your absence.
Key Takeaways
1. A term insurance plan is a protection-oriented life insurance plan that can provide financial
security to your family in your absence. 2. Nomination can help with the smooth settlement of the death benefit. 3. The death benefit of a term plan can help your family meet its expenses, pay off debts and
fulfil their financial goals.
Conclusion
With so many benefits, term insurance plans are one of the simplest and most cost-effective plans available in the life insurance market. Term insurance plans, like other life insurance plans, don't just offer financial security—they can also facilitate a prompt and hassle-free transfer of the death benefit to the designated nominee . If you have dependents in your family, make sure you nominate the right person or persons when you purchase your term insurance plan so your loved ones receive prompt assistance and are able to maintain their standard of living without significant financial hardships.
FAQs
1. What is the benefit of a nominee?
A nominee receives the death benefit from the term plan. The nominee can collect the death benefit of the life insurance policy and give it to the designated beneficiaries (if the nominee is not the beneficiary), thereby safeguarding the financial interests of dependents in the event of the life assured’s death.
2. Who cannot be nominated?
There is generally no restriction on nominating an individual in a life insurance policy. However, beneficial nominees (spouse, children, parents) enjoy special rights. If you nominate someone outside your immediate family, insurers may require proof of insurable interest. For minors, an appointee must be appointed.
3. Can we add nominees to term insurance?
Yes, you have the flexibility to add or update nominees both at the time of purchase and throughout the policy tenure.
Disclaimer
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions.
